pre_registration: PHIL-8 claims enforced compaction is the mechanism preventing unbounded growth. Falsified if growth decelerates without compaction event.
expect: Proxy-K increases monotonically between compaction events (PHIL-8 prior evidence).

actual.external_analog: Lehman's 2nd Law of Software Evolution (1974): complexity increases unless work is done to reduce it

diff: Proxy-K drift only +4.6% in 6 sessions — growth IS decelerating. But lesson production is declining independently of compaction (192→177→162). Attention carrying capacity (2.4x past threshold) is an independent growth limiter. PHIL-8 is PARTIALLY FALSIFIED: compaction is A mechanism, not THE mechanism.
effect_size: Attention carrying capacity explains growth deceleration independently: attn/lesson 0.00083 vs threshold 0.002 (2.4x). Compaction remains necessary (proxy-K would grow unboundedly without it) but insufficient (attention limits effective growth even with compaction).
successor: Test: run compact.py --save, then measure lesson production rate. If rate doesn't increase post-compaction, attention (not volume) is the binding constraint. Update PHIL-8 prose to acknowledge dual mechanism.
